title: TUI outbound_record stores coap_code which may contain internal protocol details
description:
File: python/src/lichen/tui/native.py
Lines: 403-415

The outbound_record function stores protocol details in the raw dict:

    def outbound_record(draft: MessageDraft, result: SendResult) -> MessageRecord:
        return MessageRecord(
            raw={
                "to": draft.to,
                "body": draft.body,
                "state": result.state.value,
                "coap_code": result.coap_code,  # Protocol detail
            },
            ...
        )

The coap_code (e.g., "2.01", "4.04") is internal CoAP protocol information. Storing it in the message raw dict:
1. Leaks protocol implementation details to the user-facing layer
2. Creates coupling between the TUI and CoAP transport

If the transport layer changes (e.g., to HTTP or custom protocol), this code would need updating.

Impact: Minor layering violation - not a correctness bug but makes future refactoring harder.

Fix: Store only user-facing status information, not protocol-level codes.
close_reason: Fixed by removing coap_code from synthetic MessageRecord.raw in outbound_record; tests and lint pass; committed and pushed
